rationale: Keep: the cited paper appears methodologically central rather than incidental, since it develops probabilistic subset-conjunctive/consideration-set choice models with latent-class heterogeneity, while the citing paper’s core contribution is also a process model of endogenous information-set usage alongside preference heterogeneity. The overlap in constructs (information usage/screening, two-stage consideration-choice logic, heterogeneous choice modeling) and shared empirical context (including personal-computer choice data in both notes) suggests a plausible theory/mechanism or benchmark connection that warrants full relation judgment.
